cards.text: When you come to the land that the LORD your God is giving you... you may indeed set a king over you whom the LORD your God will choose.
cards.connection: Contrasts the biblical view of kingship as a servant role with the Egyptian view of the Pharaoh as a divine god on earth.
cards.cueContext: Used in the comparison between Pharaonic and Biblical Kingship.

cards.text: But the LORD afflicted Pharaoh and his house with great plagues because of Sarai, Abram's wife.
cards.connection: Shows an early confrontation demonstrating that Yahweh's power over the world's superpower.
cards.cueContext: Displayed during the discussion of Abraham's sojourn in Egypt.

cards.text: Now there arose a new king over Egypt, who did not know Joseph.
cards.connection: Signals Egypt's willful forgetting of God's mercy through Joseph, leading directly to the oppression of Israel.
cards.cueContext: Used when discussing the darkness of the New Kingdom.

cards.text: Then all the inhabitants of Egypt shall know that I am the LORD. Because you have been a staff of reed to the house of Israel.
cards.connection: A prophetic verdict illustrating Egypt's decline into a broken and unreliable power.
cards.cueContext: Shown during the discussion of Egypt's Late Period.
